---
title: "FAQ — Transformer Models Reference"
format: html
---

## Models and Tools Referenced in the Transformers Chapter

### BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers)

- **Family:** Encoder-only
- **Training objective:** Masked Language Modeling (MLM) — randomly masks tokens and trains the model to predict them using both left and right context.
- **Attention mask:** Bidirectional (full) — every token attends to every other token.
- **Goal:** Produce rich contextual representations of text for downstream tasks (classification, named entity recognition, question answering).
- **Reference:** Devlin et al., Google, 2018.

---

### RoBERTa (Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach)

- **Family:** Encoder-only
- **Training objective:** MLM (same as BERT), but with more data, longer training, dynamic masking, and no next-sentence prediction task.
- **Goal:** Improve BERT's performance by optimising the pretraining procedure rather than the architecture.
- **Reference:** Liu et al., Facebook AI, 2019.

---

### GPT / GPT-2 / GPT-3 / GPT-4 (Generative Pre-trained Transformer)

- **Family:** Decoder-only
- **Training objective:** Causal Language Modeling (CLM) — predict the next token given all preceding tokens.
- **Attention mask:** Causal (lower-triangular) — a token can only attend to past tokens, not future ones.
- **Goal:** Autoregressive text generation; the basis for conversational agents like ChatGPT.
- **Vocabulary:** GPT-2/3/4 use Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) with $|V| \approx 50{,}000$ tokens.
- **Reference:** Radford et al. (GPT-2, 2019); Brown et al. (GPT-3, 2020), OpenAI.

---

### LLaMA (Large Language Model Meta AI)

- **Family:** Decoder-only
- **Training objective:** CLM (same family as GPT).
- **Goal:** An open-weight, efficient large language model; demonstrates that smaller models trained on more data can match larger closed models.
- **Reference:** Touvron et al., Meta AI, 2023.

---

### T5 (Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer)

- **Family:** Encoder-Decoder
- **Training objective:** Conditional / Seq2Seq Language Modeling — predict an output sequence given an input sequence.
- **Attention mask:** Encoder uses full (bidirectional) attention; decoder uses causal attention plus cross-attention to the encoder output.
- **Goal:** Unify all NLP tasks as text-to-text problems (translation, summarisation, classification, Q&A).
- **Reference:** Raffel et al., Google, 2019.

---

### mT5 (Multilingual T5)

- **Family:** Encoder-Decoder
- **Structure:** Same architecture as T5, pre-trained on a multilingual corpus covering 101 languages.
- **Goal:** Extend T5's text-to-text framework to multilingual settings.
- **Reference:** Xue et al., Google, 2021.

---

### BART (Bidirectional and Auto-Regressive Transformers)

- **Family:** Encoder-Decoder
- **Training objective:** Seq2Seq reconstruction — the encoder sees a corrupted version of the input (masking, deletion, permutation); the decoder reconstructs the original.
- **Goal:** Particularly strong for generation tasks such as summarisation and translation, combining BERT-like encoding with GPT-like decoding.
- **Reference:** Lewis et al., Facebook AI, 2019.

---

### Longformer

- **Family:** Encoder-only (variant)
- **Key idea:** **Sparse attention** — combines a sliding-window local attention with global attention on selected tokens, reducing complexity from $O(n^2)$ to $O(n)$.
- **Goal:** Handle long documents (thousands of tokens) that are intractable for standard full attention.
- **Reference:** Beltagy et al., Allen AI, 2020.

---

### BigBird

- **Family:** Encoder-only (variant)
- **Key idea:** **Sparse attention** — combines random attention, local (window) attention, and global attention tokens.
- **Goal:** Extend transformer capabilities to long sequences while preserving theoretical expressiveness of full attention.
- **Reference:** Zaheer et al., Google, 2020.

---

### Flash Attention

- **Type:** Algorithmic / hardware-aware attention implementation (not a model family).
- **Key idea:** Reorders attention computation to minimise slow HBM memory reads/writes on GPUs, computing exact attention without materialising the full $n \times n$ attention matrix.
- **Goal:** Make standard self-attention faster and more memory-efficient, enabling longer context windows without approximation.
- **Reference:** Dao et al., 2022.
